decisions.summary: Forge detection is by URL path pattern, not host: /pull/(\d+) => github, /-/merge_requests/(\d+) => gitlab. Supports self-hosted GitLab. New parseArtifactRef() lives in src/lib/forge.ts.

Add a real `forge TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'github'` column to pending_auto_merges (SCHEMA_VERSION 53->54) rather than overloading prNumber. Back-fills existing rows to 'github'. Makes the row self-describing so a GitLab iid in prNumber is never misread as a GitHub PR number.

GitLab eligibility is a focused injectable helper inside auto-merge-eligibility.ts, NOT a unified ForgeAdapter.getArtifactState() abstraction. The shared-abstraction convergence with PAN-933 is deferred.

GitLab MR mergeability uses `detailed_merge_status === 'mergeable'` as primary signal, with state==='opened' + draft pre-checks and merge_status==='can_be_merged' fallback. glab field names are an implementation checkpoint (confirm against a real MYN MR; fallback documented).

Execution is NOT re-wired: triggerMerge (workspaces.ts) already resolves primaryForge from the merge set and merges via getForgeAdapter(forge).mergeReviewArtifact (glab mr merge for gitlab). The auto-merge executor delegates to triggerMerge. Issue work-item 3 is a verification + missing forge.ts test, not new code.

hazards.summary: isAutoMergeEligible fires at flywheel.ts:325 BEFORE parsePrNumber at :333. It is GitHub-only and would 422 a GitLab MR first. Fixing only parsePrNumber (as the issue brief framed the 'blocking gap') does NOT unblock MIN-831.
hazards.mitigation: WI-4 makes eligibility forge-aware; WI-3 makes the schedule parse forge-aware. BOTH must merge for the end-to-end fix. Each lands without regressing GitHub.

hazards.summary: glab `mr view -F json` field names (detailed_merge_status, merge_status, state, draft, has_conflicts) are assumed snake_case from go-gitlab; the installed GitLab instance may not populate detailed_merge_status.
hazards.mitigation: WI-4 implementation checkpoint: confirm fields via `glab mr view 62 -R eltmon/mind-your-now -F json | jq 'keys'`; documented fallback to state+merge_status+has_conflicts.

hazards.summary: The `glab` eligibility call is server-reachable; execSync would freeze the dashboard event loop (PAN-70).
hazards.mitigation: WI-4 uses promisified execFile only; an injectable dep keeps it unit-testable with no real glab call.

hazards.summary: Schema column add must stay in lockstep with pending-auto-merges-schema.test.ts which pins SCHEMA_VERSION===53 and the exact column list.
hazards.mitigation: WI-2 updates the test (version 54 + 'forge' in the column list) in the same bead as the migration.

hazards.summary: GitLab eligibility needs the project path for `glab -R`; eligibility only has prUrl.
hazards.mitigation: Parse <projectPath> from https://<host>/<projectPath>/-/merge_requests/<iid>. MYN is gitlab.com; self-hosted host-credentialed routing is a NonGoal.

sessionHistory.note: Initial planning session for PAN-1887 (pan plan --auto). Verified the code: execution is already forge-aware via triggerMerge->forge adapter; the real blocking gaps are the two GitHub-only gates in the schedule path (isAutoMergeEligible + parsePrNumber). 5 beads.
